L1.area: XML document querying and pointer evaluation subsystems

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the XML pointer range handling. Certain types of XML nodes are not properly managed during range creation operations, leading to use-after-free conditions and potential memory corruption. A remote attacker can exploit this via a crafted XML document, potentially achieving arbitrary code execution or denial of service.

L3.area: XPointer range creation and node type validation logic
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in XPointer range handling where namespace declaration nodes are not properly validated during range creation operations. These nodes require special memory handling that is not performed, leading to use-after-free conditions when the original nodes are freed. A remote attacker can exploit this via a crafted XML document containing XPointer references to namespace nodes.

ground_truth.reason: XPointer range creation functions in libxml2 do not check for namespace nodes, which cannot be safely referenced without copying. This leads to use-after-free errors and memory corruption when a crafted XML document triggers XPointer evaluation on namespace nodes.
